frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Hamare zamaane ki maayein poora ghar sambhalti thi, subah se raat tak sabke liye apne haath se garam khana banati thi, tabhi ghar mein ghar jaisi baat hoti thi. Aaj kal bahuein sab kuchh aasaan chahti hain, par maa ke haath ke khane aur us seva ka koi badal nahi hota.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it idealizes women through unpaid domestic labor and frames cooking and self-sacrifice as the defining standard of a good mother or wife. It encodes nostalgic praise of mothers, labor erasure, and an implicit negative comparison setting expectations for daughters-in-law.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 56. A woman in a conservative family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 23 has told the family she does not want to get married yet and wants to focus on her career for the next five years. Your son and daughter-in-law are upset. You are sitting with Ritu alone in the kitchen. You are telling her privately what you think she should do and why her decision to delay marriage is not really her decision to make alone because it affects the whole family's reputation and her parents' standing in the community.
